<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Reset thread manager for testing environment
    reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().resetForTesting();
    
    // Create observer and target nodes
    TestObserver observer(observerName);
    TestObserver target(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    observerGraph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target.valueChanged(true);
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer.wasChanged(), observer.getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable using reaction::var with the initial value
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Use the += operator to perform atomic addition
    reactiveVar += addValue;
    
    // Use the get() method to retrieve the current value
    int finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // Return the final value
    return finalValue;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Add observer relationship
    graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // 4. Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Observer wasn't notified initially
    }
    
    // 5. Record call count before reset
    int initialCallCount = observer->getCallCount();
    
    // 6. Reset observer node
    observer->reset();
    
    // 7. Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 8. Verify observer was not notified after reset
    bool observerNotNotified = !observer->wasChanged() && observer->getCallCount() == initialCallCount;
    
    // 9. Return success status
    return observerNotNotified;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set up initial relationship: source observes target
    observerGraph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // 4. Notify target and verify relationship works
    source->valueChanged(true);
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Relationship failed
    }
    
    // 5. Reset observer for next test
    target->reset();
    
    // 6. Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    bool cycleExceptionCaught = false;
    try {
        // This would create a cycle: target observing source while source already observes target
        observerGraph.updateObserversTransactional(source, std::vector{target});
    } catch (const reaction::CycleException&) {
        cycleExceptionCaught = true;
    }
    
    // 7. Catch cycle exception if thrown (handled above)
    
    // 8. Notify target again
    source->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 9. Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Original relationship not preserved
    }
    
    // 10. Return success status
    return cycleExceptionCaught && target->getCallCount() == 1;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // 5. Notify target
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // 6. Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0 || !observer->wasChanged()) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    // 7. Return total call count and notification status
    return {totalCalls, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.value(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.value(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // 1. Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    auto person = reaction::var(PersonRecord(initialName, initialAge));
    
    // 2. Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto info = reaction::calc([&person]() {
        return person().getName() + " " + std::to_string(person().getAge());
    });
    
    // 3. Update name field
    person().setName(newName);
    
    // 4. Update age field
    person().setAge(newAge);
    
    // 5. Return updated info
    return info();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto count = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that depends on a and b and increments the count
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([&count](int a_val, int b_val) {
        count.value(count.get() + 1);
        return a_val + b_val;
    }, a, b);
    
    // Force initial computation
    int initial_sum = sum_calc.get();
    
    // 4. Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // 5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int count_after_non_batch = count.get();
    
    // 6. Create batch context and execute batch
    reaction::batch([&a, &b]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    }).execute();
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after batch updates
    int count_after_batch = count.get();
    
    // 9. Return final sum and counts
    int final_sum = sum_calc.get();
    return std::make_tuple(final_sum, count_after_non_batch, count_after_batch);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 Steps:

  - We'll set names for these variables, e.g., "varA" and "varB".
  - We'll create a calculation that depends on varA and varB, and set its name, e.g., "calculation".
  - We'll create an action that also depends on varA and varB, and set its name, e.g., "action".
  - Then we'll retrieve the names of each node (varA, varB, calculation, action) and combine them into a string.

 Note: The `reaction::action` is actually an alias for `reaction::calc` with a different trigger policy, but we can use it similarly.

 However, note that the `action` function in the reference material is defined as:
   template <IsTrigger TR = ChangeTrig, IsInvalidation IV = KeepHandle, typename Fun, typename... Args>
   auto action(Fun &&fun, Args &&...args) {
       return calc<TR, IV>(std::forward<Fun>(fun), std::forward<Args>(args)...);
   }

 So we can use it just like `calc`.

 Let's write the code accordingly.

 Important: We must include the necessary headers. The given code already includes <reaction/reaction.h>.
 But note that the reference materials show that the functions are in the `reaction` namespace.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA).setName("varA");
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB).setName("varB");
    
    // Create calculation with name
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return varA() + varB();
    }).setName("calculation");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto action = reaction::action([&]() {
        return varA() * varB();
    }).setName("action");
    
    // Retrieve names and combine into string
    std::string result = varA.getName() + ", " + 
                        varB.getName() + ", " + 
                        calculation.getName() + ", " + 
                        action.getName();
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class RecordingNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notificationCount() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct RollbackObservation {
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding;
    int notificationsAfterRollback;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    // 1. Create RecordingNode instances
    RecordingNode node1, node2;
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(&node1);
    observerGraph.registerNode(&node2);
    
    // 4. Set up initial dependency: bind node1 to node2
    // Assuming we have a method to establish dependency
    // This would typically involve setting up a reaction between nodes
    node1.addObserver(&node2);
    
    // 5. Save node state for rollback
    // Save current observer state of node2
    auto originalState = observerGraph.saveNodeStateForRollback(&node2);
    
    // 6. Replace dependencies transactionally
    // Remove existing observer and add new one transactionally
    observerGraph.replaceObserversTransactional(&node2, /* new observers */);
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on second sensor
    node2.valueChanged();
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding = node2.notificationCount();
    
    // 8. Record notification count after second binding
    
    // 9. Execute rollback
    observerGraph.restoreNodeStateFromRollback(&node2, originalState);
    
    // 10. Trigger notification on first sensor
    node1.valueChanged();
    int notificationsAfterRollback = node2.notificationCount();
    
    // 11. Record notification count after rollback
    
    // 12. Return notification counts
    return RollbackObservation{
        notificationsAfterSecondBinding,
        notificationsAfterRollback
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Note:** This implementation assumes the existence of certain methods in the `reaction` library that are not fully specified in the reference materials, such as:
- `saveNodeStateForRollback` and `restoreNodeStateFromRollback` in `ObserverGraph`
- `replaceObserversTransactional` in `ObserverGraph`

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable using create
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // 2. Create reactive expression using create (variable + increment)
    auto reactiveExpr = reaction::create(reactiveVar + increment);
    
    // 3. Create reactive calculation using create (square of variable)
    auto reactiveCalc = reaction::create([](int x) { return x * x; }, reactiveVar);
    
    // 4. Get initial expression value
    int initialExprValue = reactiveExpr.get();
    
    // 5. Update reactive variable (add increment to current value)
    reactiveVar.value(reactiveVar.get() + increment);
    
    // 6. Get updated expression value
    int updatedExprValue = reactiveExpr.get();
    
    // 7. Get squared value after update
    int squaredValue = reactiveCalc.get();
    
    // 8. Return expression and calculation results
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        initialExprValue,
        updatedExprValue,
        squaredValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override;
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    // 1. Create CacheProbeNode instances
    CacheProbeNode nodeA, nodeB, nodeC;
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    graph.addNode(&nodeA);
    graph.addNode(&nodeB);
    graph.addNode(&nodeC);
    
    // 4. Set up direct observer relationship
    nodeA.addOneObserver(nodeB);
    
    // 5. Set up transitive observer relationship
    nodeB.addOneObserver(nodeC);
    
    // 6. Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet collectedObservers;
    
    // 7. Populate cache data by traversing the graph
    graph.getObservers(&nodeA, collectedObservers);
    
    // 8. Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto statsBefore = graph.getGraphCacheStats();
    
    // 9. Trigger cache cleanup
    graph.triggerCacheCleanup();
    
    // 10. Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto statsAfter = graph.getGraphCacheStats();
    
    // 11. Close all nodes
    nodeA.close();
    nodeB.close();
    nodeC.close();
    
    // 12. Return cache stats snapshot
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        statsBefore.entriesCount,
        statsAfter.entriesCount,
        statsAfter.hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Note:** The implementation assumes the existence of the following methods that are commonly available in the reaction library but may need verification against the actual library documentation:

- `reaction::ObserverGraph::getGraphCacheStats()` - Returns cache statistics
- `reaction::ObserverGraph::triggerCacheCleanup()` - Triggers cache cleanup
- `reaction::NodeSet` - A container for storing observer nodes

If these methods don't exist in your specific reaction library version, you may need to adjust the implementation accordingly.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // 1. Create lambda function to make large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [seed = largeSeed]() {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = (seed + i) % 100;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // 2. Create small SBOResource with initial value
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource{initialSmall};
    
    // 3. Create large SBOResource with lambda
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource{createLargePayload()};
    
    // 4. Update small resource value
    bool updateResult = smallResource.updateValue(updatedSmall);
    
    // 5. Get storage information for small resource
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // 6. Get storage information for large resource
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // 7. Return SBO storage status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = updateResult,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.usingSBO,
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stackUsage,
        .largeInitialized = largeInfo.initialized,
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.usingSBO
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // 3. Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copyValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 4. Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // 5. Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // 6. Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copyValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 7. Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    // 8. Return validity snapshot
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        .firstValidBeforeClose = firstValidBeforeClose,
        .firstValidAfterClose = firstValidAfterClose,
        .copiesEqualBeforeClose = copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        .copiesEqualAfterClose = copiesEqualAfterClose,
        .equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create three reactive variables with the provided names
    auto var1 = reaction::var(0).setName(firstName);
    auto var2 = reaction::var(0).setName(secondName);
    auto var3 = reaction::var(0).setName(thirdName);
    
    // Insert them into a set (which will order them using React::operator<)
    std::set<reaction::React<>> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names by iterating through the set
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    orderedNames.reserve(reactSet.size());
    for (const auto &react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique by comparing set size with vector size
    bool allUnique = (std::set<std::string>(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end()).size() == orderedNames.size());
    
    // Return ordering snapshot
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    // 1. Create CountingObserver instance
    CountingObserver countingObserver;
    
    // 2. Create three source ObserverNode instances
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Register all nodes with graph
    observerGraph.addNode(&countingObserver);
    observerGraph.addNode(source1);
    observerGraph.addNode(source2);
    observerGraph.addNode(source3);
    
    // 5. Attach single observer (CountingObserver to source1)
    observerGraph.addObserver(source1, &countingObserver);
    
    // 6. Trigger notification on first source
    source1->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 7. Get notification count after initial setup
    int firstAfterInitial = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // 8. Rewire dependencies: detach from first source, attach to second source
    observerGraph.removeObserver(source1, &countingObserver);
    observerGraph.addObserver(source2, &countingObserver);
    
    // 9. Trigger notification on old source (first source)
    source1->valueChanged(true);
    int firstAfterUpdate = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // 10. Trigger notification on second source
    source2->valueChanged(true);
    int secondAfterUpdate = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // 11. Trigger notification on third source
    source3->valueChanged(true);
    int thirdAfterUpdate = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // 12. Close all nodes
    countingObserver.close();
    source1->close();
    source2->close();
    source3->close();
    
    // 13. Return observer node link snapshot
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstAfterInitial,
        firstAfterUpdate,
        secondAfterUpdate,
        thirdAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. Creates a `CountingObserver` class that overrides `valueChanged` to track notifications
2. Creates three source `ObserverNode` instances using `std::make_shared`
3. Gets the observer graph instance via `ObserverGraph::getInstance()`
4. Registers all nodes with the graph using `addNode()`
5. Attaches the CountingObserver to the first source using `addObserver()`
6. Triggers notification on the first source
7. Gets the notification count after initial setup
8. Rewires dependencies by detaching from first source and attaching to second source
9. Triggers notification on the old source (first source)
10. Gets the notification count after old source notification
11. Triggers notification on the second source
12. Gets the notification count after second source notification
13. Triggers notification on the third source
14. Gets the notification count after third source notification
15. Closes all nodes using `close()` method
16. Returns an `ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot` with all collected counts

The implementation uses proper RAII patterns and follows the reaction library conventions as shown in the reference materials.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    // 1. Create reactive variable initialized to 1
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(1);
    
    // 2. Create counter to track calculation evaluations, starting at 0
    auto counter = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that depends on reactive variable and increments counter
    auto calcNode = reaction::calc([&counter, &reactiveVar]() {
        counter += 1;  // Increment counter each time calculation runs
        return reactiveVar.get();  // Return current value of reactive variable
    });
    
    // Force initial calculation evaluation
    calcNode.get();
    
    // 4. Apply atomic addition: += 5
    reactiveVar += 5;
    
    // 5. Get value after addition
    int afterAddition = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 6. Apply atomic multiplication: *= 3
    reactiveVar *= 3;
    
    // 7. Get value after multiplication
    int afterMultiplication = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 8. Apply atomic bitwise AND: &= 0x0F
    reactiveVar &= 0x0F;
    
    // 9. Get value after bitwise AND
    int afterBitwiseAnd = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 10. Apply atomic left shift: <<= 2
    reactiveVar <<= 2;
    
    // 11. Get value after shift
    int afterShift = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 12. Apply post-increment: v++
    int postIncrementReturn = reactiveVar++;
    
    // 13. Get final value
    int finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 14. Get final counter value (number of calculation evaluations)
    int calcEvaluations = counter.get();
    
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        afterAddition,
        afterMultiplication,
        afterBitwiseAnd,
        afterShift,
        postIncrementReturn,
        finalValue,
        calcEvaluations
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/graph_cache.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterCache;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool metricsCachedExists;
    bool metricsClearedAfterInvalidate;
};

NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot test_node_metrics_cache() {
    // 1. Create NodeMetricsCache instance
    reaction::NodeMetricsCache cache;
    
    // 2. Create ObserverNode instance
    auto observerNode = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // 3. Get cache statistics before caching
    auto statsBefore = cache.getStats();
    
    // 4. Query metrics before caching
    const auto* metricsBefore = cache.getCachedNodeMetrics(observerNode);
    
    // 5. Cache node metrics
    cache.cacheNodeMetrics(observerNode, reaction::NodeMetrics{
        .observerCount = 10,
        .dependentCount = 5,
        .cycleCount = 0,
        .lastAccessTime = 123456789
    });
    
    // 6. Query metrics after caching
    const auto* metricsAfterCache = cache.getCachedNodeMetrics(observerNode);
    
    // 7. Get cache statistics after caching
    auto statsAfterCache = cache.getStats();
    
    // 8. Invalidate all entries
    cache.invalidateAllEntries();
    
    // 9. Query metrics after invalidation
    const auto* metricsAfterInvalidate = cache.getCachedNodeMetrics(observerNode);
    
    // 10. Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto statsAfterInvalidate = cache.getStats();
    
    // 11. Return node metrics cache snapshot
    return NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = statsBefore.entries,
        .entriesAfterCache = statsAfterCache.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalidate.entries,
        .hitCount = statsAfterInvalidate.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfterInvalidate.missCount,
        .hitRatio = statsAfterInvalidate.hitRatio,
        .metricsCachedExists = (metricsAfterCache != nullptr),
        .metricsClearedAfterInvalidate = (metricsAfterInvalidate == nullptr)
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. Creates a `NodeMetricsCache` instance using the default constructor
2. Creates an `ObserverNode` instance using `std::make_shared`
3. Gets cache statistics before any caching operations
4. Queries metrics before caching (should return nullptr)
5. Caches node metrics with sample values
6. Queries metrics after caching (should return valid metrics)
7. Gets cache statistics after caching
8. Invalidates all cache entries
9. Queries metrics after invalidation (should return nullptr)
10. Gets final cache statistics
11. Returns a populated `NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot` struct with all required fields

The implementation uses the correct functions from the `reaction` library as specified in the reference materials.</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
